title: Arrest Rate by Crime Type
finding_preview: Arrest rates vary significantly — GAMBLING highest (100.0%), PUBLIC PEACE VIOLATION lowest (48.0%).
finding_full: Arrest rates vary significantly by crime type across 51,996 records (2019–2025). GAMBLING has the highest arrest rate (100.0%), while PUBLIC PEACE VIOLATION has the lowest (48.0%). This reflects differences in investigation difficulty and evidence requirements.

title: Arrest Rate by Year
finding_full: The arrest rate fluctuates between 11.8% (2023) and 21.2% (2019) across 2019–2025. 2019: 21.2%, 2020: 15.9%, 2021: 13.2%, 2022: 12.0%, 2023: 11.8%, 2024: 14.4%, 2025: 15.9%. Year-over-year variation suggests shifting enforcement priorities.

title: Arrest Rate by District
finding_full: Arrest rates vary by district. District 11 has the highest arrest rate (30.1%), while District 2 has the lowest (10.8%). Top 5: D11 (30.1%), D10 (23.2%), D7 (17.9%), D15 (17.4%), D1 (16.7%). Bottom 5: D2 (10.8%), D17 (10.6%), D12 (9.9%), D19 (9.3%), D31 (0.0%).

title: Arrest Rate by Location Type
finding_preview: POLICE FACILITY / VEHICLE PARKING LOT highest arrest rate (51.4%), ALLEY lowest (26.0%).
finding_full: Arrest rates vary by location type. POLICE FACILITY / VEHICLE PARKING LOT has the highest arrest rate (51.4%), followed by CTA STATION (39.8%). Locations like ALLEY have lower rates (26.0%). This reflects differences in witness availability and police presence.

title: Domestic vs Non-Domestic Crimes
finding_preview: Domestic crimes: 19.4% of total, 14.4% arrest rate. BATTERY dominates domestic incidents (51%).
finding_full: Domestic crimes account for 19.4% of all 51,996 incidents (2019–2025). They have a 14.4% arrest rate vs 15.0% for non-domestic. BATTERY is the dominant domestic crime type (51% of domestic incidents).

title: Annual Crime Volume
finding_full: Annual crime volume varies across 2019–2025. 2019 recorded the highest volume (2019: 7,428 incidents), while 2019 had the lowest (7,428). 2019: 7,428, 2020: 7,428, 2021: 7,428, 2022: 7,428, 2023: 7,428, 2024: 7,428, 2025: 7,428.

title: Most Common Crime Types
finding_preview: THEFT is the most common type (22.1% of all incidents).
finding_full: The top 10 crime types account for 91% of all 51,996 incidents. THEFT leads with 22.1%, followed by BATTERY (18.0%) and CRIMINAL DAMAGE (11.1%). The long tail includes 30 total crime types.

title: Hotspot Stability Over Time
finding_preview: Top 3 districts (11.0, 6.0, 1.0) remain highly stable across years.
finding_full: District rankings are highly stable across 2019–2025. The top 3 districts (11.0, 6.0, 1.0) remain consistent, suggesting persistent geographic concentration of crime. This stability implies resource allocation based on historical data remains relevant.

title: Month-by-Location Heatmap
finding_preview: Summer crime volume is 1.2x winter. STREET locations show strongest seasonal variation.
finding_full: Crime volume shows strong seasonal patterns across location types. Summer months (Jun–Aug) average 1.2x the volume of winter months (Dec–Feb). STREET locations show the strongest seasonal variation, while RESIDENCE locations are more stable year-round.

title: Crime by Day of Week
finding_preview: Fri is the peak crime day. Weekend arrest rate (15.1%) vs weekday (14.8%).
finding_full: Crime volume peaks on Fri and is lowest on Tue. Weekend days (Sat/Sun) have 15.1% arrest rate vs 14.8% on weekdays. This suggests weekend crimes may involve more public-space offenses where immediate arrest is more likely.

title: Crime by Hour of Day
finding_preview: Peak hour: 0:00. Night hours (10pm–2am) account for 11,115 incidents.
finding_full: Crime volume peaks at 0:00 and is lowest at 5:00. Late evening/night hours (10pm–2am) account for 11,115 incidents (21.4%), while midday (10am–2pm) accounts for 12,624 (24.3%).

title: THEFT Location Distribution
finding_preview: THEFT occurs most on STREET (23.8%). Top 3 locations account for 45%.
finding_full: THEFT (11,500 incidents) is concentrated in specific location types. STREET accounts for 23.8%, followed by APARTMENT (12.3%) and SMALL RETAIL STORE (8.7%). The top 3 locations represent 45% of all theft incidents.

title: Crime Type Mix by Top Districts
finding_preview: Top districts have different crime profiles: D6: BATTERY (20%), D8: THEFT (20%).
finding_full: Each of the top 5 districts by volume has a distinct crime profile. D6: BATTERY (20%), D8: THEFT (20%), D11: BATTERY (21%), D4: BATTERY (20%), D12: THEFT (31%). This heterogeneity suggests that policing strategies should be tailored to each district's dominant crime pattern rather than using uniform approaches.

title: Domestic vs Non-Domestic Arrest by Type
finding_preview: BATTERY: domestic arrest rate significantly higher than non-domestic.
finding_full: Comparing arrest rates for domestic vs non-domestic incidents by crime type: BATTERY: domestic 19% vs non-domestic 14%; ASSAULT: domestic 12% vs non-domestic 11%; OTHER OFFENSE: domestic 11% vs non-domestic 20%; CRIMINAL DAMAGE: domestic 7% vs non-domestic 4%; THEFT: domestic 5% vs non-domestic 7%. Domestic BATTERY has notably higher arrest rates, likely because the victim is present and can identify the suspect.

title: Crime Trend by Type (2019 vs Latest)
finding_preview: Key trends: THEFT decreased 2%, BATTERY decreased 7%, CRIMINAL DAMAGE increased 3%.
finding_full: Comparing the earliest to latest year for top crime types: THEFT decreased 2%, BATTERY decreased 7%, CRIMINAL DAMAGE increased 3%, ASSAULT increased 13%, DECEPTIVE PRACTICE decreased 13%. These trends reveal shifting crime patterns that may reflect changes in enforcement, reporting, or socioeconomic conditions.

title: Seasonal Crime Pattern
finding_preview: Peak month: Jul (4,841). Summer months are 1.2x winter.
finding_full: Crime volume follows a clear seasonal pattern. Jul is the peak month (4,841 incidents), while Feb is the lowest (3,681). Summer months (Jun–Aug) collectively have 1.2x the volume of winter months (Dec–Feb), consistent with known patterns of outdoor crime increasing in warm weather.
